source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/03_설계개발 출력/10_User manual/[HnX-P1] User Manual(EU) (A-IFU-HNX-EU, Rev 0.5, 260429).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: User manual and service information for the HnX-P1 battery-powered portable X-ray system, including intended use, safety warnings, regulatory standards, technical specifications, operation, maintenance, calibration, and product information.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Provided to the user along with the HnX-P1 and intended only for the HnX-P1; users should read the manual before operation and follow safety precautions, operation, maintenance, and service instructions.
keyPoints: The HnX-P1 is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.
The device is intended for professional healthcare facilities and indoor controlled environments where radiation safety protocols and distance requirements are maintained.
The device emits ionizing radiation and is restricted to qualified healthcare professionals or trained service personnel.
The HnX-P1 is not intended for pediatric use, mammography, dental radiography, fluoroscopy, or therapy.
The regulatory section lists IEC, EN, FCC, KS, FDA guidance, and 21 CFR radiation performance standards addressed by the device.
The device has software/firmware HnX_P1 version 00.13 and Bluetooth Low Energy thumbnail preview transmission using module BoT-nLE521.

concepts.name: Portable X-ray System
concepts.evidence: The HnX-P1 is a portable X-ray System.

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The HnX-P1 is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.

concepts.name: Contra-indications
concepts.evidence: Pregnancy: The device is contraindicated for use in the abdominal and pelvic regions of women who are pregnant or may be pregnant.

concepts.name: Pediatric warning
concepts.evidence: The device is not intended for use in the Pediatric population, which includes Children (patients aged 2 years and above) and Infants (patients under 2 years old).

concepts.name: Regulatory Information
concepts.evidence: 3. Regulatory Information 3.1 Medical Equipment Safety Standards

concepts.name: EMC safety
concepts.evidence: HnX-P1 requires special precautions regarding EMC and needs to be operated only by authorized personnel and put into service according to EMC information provided in the user manual.

concepts.name: Radiation safety
concepts.evidence: When X-ray is being irradiated, the operator shall be stand at least 1.8meters(6feet) from the patient, the X-ray tube, and out of the useful beam.

concepts.name: Unauthorized use controls
concepts.evidence: This device requires facility-implemented access controls. Failure to implement proper procedural safeguards violates radiation safety regulations

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: The error in the indicated value of the X-ray tube voltage, in any combination of loading factors, shall be not greater than 10%.

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 is a portable X-ray system.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 is a portable X-ray System.

evidence.claim: The user manual only pertains to HnX-P1 and is provided with the device.
evidence.evidenceText: This User Manual is provided to the user along with the HnX-P1. This User Manual only pertains to the HnX-P1 and does not serve for any other products of the company.

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co., Ltd. is identified as the manufacturer.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer: H&abyz Co., Ltd. 2-dong, 41-16, Cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-eup, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, 17118, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: Javitech Consulting GmbH is identified as the European Authorized Representative.
evidence.evidenceText: European Authorized Representative Javitech Consulting GmbH Sachsenhausener Str. 16, 65824 Schwalbach a. Ts. Germany

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 is intended to generate radiographic images of human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 is intended for use in professional healthcare facilities under controlled indoor radiation-safety conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 is intended for use in professional healthcare facilities, such as hospitals, clinics, and emergency departments. The device is limited to indoor operation within controlled environments where radiation safety protocols and distance requirements are maintained.

evidence.claim: The target population is adult patients, with pregnancy limitations requiring clinical justification by a physician.
evidence.evidenceText: Age: Adult patients. Patient State: No specific limitations apply except for pregnant women, for whom use must be clinically justified by a physician.

evidence.claim: The device is contraindicated for abdominal and pelvic use in women who are pregnant or may be pregnant.
evidence.evidenceText: Pregnancy: The device is contraindicated for use in the abdominal and pelvic regions of women who are pregnant or may be pregnant.

evidence.claim: The device is contraindicated for non-extremity examinations in bariatric patients defined as BMI 40 kg/m^2.
evidence.evidenceText: Bariatric Patients: The device is strictly contraindicated for any examination other than the extremities (i.e., limb imaging) in bariatric patients (defined as BMI 40 kg/m^2).

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Mammography: This device is not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The device is not intended for pediatric use.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is not intended for use in the Pediatric population, which includes Children (patients aged 2 years and above) and Infants (patients under 2 years old).

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 is not intended for fluoroscopic use or other associated applications.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 is designed for radiographic uses only and not intended for fluoroscopic use or other associated applications.

evidence.claim: The device does not apply to dental radiography.
evidence.evidenceText: The device does not apply to mammography and dental radiography.

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 is intended for diagnosis and not therapy.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 should be used for the diagnosis purpose and is not intended for therapy of any kind.

evidence.claim: The device emits ionizing radiation and unauthorized operation may cause inappropriate exposure.
evidence.evidenceText: This device emits ionizing radiation and lacks built-in user authentication. Unauthorized operation may result in inappropriate radiation exposure to patients, operators, or bystanders

evidence.claim: The device requires facility-implemented access controls.
evidence.evidenceText: This device requires facility-implemented access controls. Failure to implement proper procedural safeguards violates radiation safety regulations

evidence.claim: Use is restricted to qualified healthcare professionals or trained service personnel.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is intended for use only by qualified healthcare professionals or trained service personnel. Use in accordance with institutional protocols and operator training.

evidence.claim: Facilities must maintain a written list of authorized operators.
evidence.evidenceText: Facilities must maintain a current written list of all authorized operators including: Full name, license number and expiration date, training completion date etc.

evidence.claim: The operator should stand at least 1.8 meters from the patient and X-ray tube and outside the useful beam during irradiation.
evidence.evidenceText: When X-ray is being irradiated, the operator shall be stand at least 1.8meters(6feet) from the patient, the X-ray tube, and out of the useful beam.

evidence.claim: During exposure, operators should wear protective clothing of at least 0.5 mm lead equivalence or 1.6 mm lead equivalence and stand behind shielding.
evidence.evidenceText: During X-ray exposure, the operator should wear a qualified protective clothing e.g. apron of min. 0.5 mm lead equivalence or 1.6mm(1/16inch) lead equivalence and stand behind the lead screen or the lead glass screen

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 is specified as Class I and IPX0 under EN 60601-1 standards.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 is specified as Class I and IPX0 equipment under EN 60601-1 standards.

evidence.claim: The regulatory classification lists Class I or internally powered ME equipment and IPX0 ingress protection.
evidence.evidenceText: Type of protection against electrical shock Class I or Internally powered ME equipment Degree of protection against ingress of water IPX0

evidence.claim: The mode of operation is continuous operation.
evidence.evidenceText: Mode of operation Continuous operation

evidence.claim: The device is not suitable for flammable anesthetic mixtures or oxygen rich environments.
evidence.evidenceText: Not suitable for use in the presence of a flammable anesthetic mixture with air or with oxygen or nitrous oxide Not suitable for use in the oxygen rich environment

evidence.claim: The regulatory section lists IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD2:2020 as a product safety standard.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1: 2005/AMD2:2020 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance

evidence.claim: The regulatory section lists IEC 60601-1-2:2014+A1:2020 as an EMC collateral standard.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-2: 2014+A1:2020 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-2: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral Standard: Electromagnetic disturbances - Requirements and tests

evidence.claim: The regulatory section lists IEC 60601-1-3:2008/AMD2:2021 for radiation protection in diagnostic X-ray equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-3:2008/ AMD2:2021 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-3: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral Standard: Radiation protection in diagnostic X-ray equipment

evidence.claim: The regulatory section lists IEC 60601-2-28:2017 for X-ray tube assemblies for medical diagnosis.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-2-28:2017 Medical electrical equipment - Part 2-28: Particular requirements for the basic safety and essential performance of X-ray tube assemblies for medical diagnosis

evidence.claim: The regulatory section lists IEC 60601-2-54:2022 for X-ray equipment for radiography and radioscopy.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-2-54:2022 Medical electrical equipment -- Part 2-54: Particular requirements for the basic safety and essential performance of X ray equipment for radiography and radioscopy

evidence.claim: The regulatory section lists IEC 62304:2006/AMD1:2015 for medical device software lifecycle processes.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62304:2006/AMD1:2015 Medical device software - Software life-cycle processes

evidence.claim: The regulatory section lists IEC 62366-1:2015/AMD1:2020 for usability engineering.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62366-1:2015/AMD1:2020 Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: The regulatory section lists EN ISO 15223-1:2021/AMD1:2025 for symbols supplied by the manufacturer.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 15223-1:2021/AMD1:2025 Medical devices — Symbols to be used with information to be supplied by the manufacturer — Part 1: General requirements

evidence.claim: The regulatory section lists FCC Sub Part C for intentional RF emission devices.
evidence.evidenceText: FCC SUB PART C This section of the FCC regulations covers the rules and requirements for devices that intentionally generate and emit radio frequency energy

evidence.claim: The regulatory section states that applicable aspects of listed 510(k) guidance documents have been addressed.
evidence.evidenceText: All applicable aspects of these guidance documents listed in these 510(k) summaries have been addressed.

evidence.claim: The device conforms to 21 CFR 1020 Subchapter J and 21 CFR 1020.30.
evidence.evidenceText: The device also conforms to the following: • 21 CFR 1020 Subchapter J: Performance Standards for Ionizing Radiation Emitting Products • 21 CFR 1020.30: Diagnostic x-ray system and their major components

evidence.claim: HnX-P1 conforms to IEC 60601-1-2:2020 for immunity and emissions.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX-P1 conforms to this IEC60601-1-2:2020 standard for both immunity and emissions.

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 software/firmware is HnX_P1 version 00.13.
evidence.evidenceText: Software(Firmware): HnX_P1 Version: 00.13

evidence.claim: The device uses Bluetooth Low Energy for thumbnail image preview transmission.
evidence.evidenceText: Bluetooth (BLE) Module BoT-nLE521 Standard Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) Frequency Range 2,402 – 2,480 MHz Function Thumbnail image preview transmission

evidence.claim: The maximum X-ray generator output is 720 W.
evidence.evidenceText: The maximum output of X-ray generator is 720W

evidence.claim: The generator tube output voltage range is 50 to 90 kV.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray Tube output voltage of generator is 50~90kV

evidence.claim: The tube focal spot is 0.5 mm x 0.5 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray Tube focal spot is 0.5㎜ x 0.5㎜

evidence.claim: The maximum output feature is 90 kV / 8 mA.
evidence.evidenceText: Maximum output is 90kV / 8mA

evidence.claim: The estimated product lifetime is up to 7 years under appropriate inspection and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: The estimated product lifetime is up to 7 years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

evidence.claim: Regular inspection should be performed at least once a year.
evidence.evidenceText: be sure to perform regular inspection at least once a year.

evidence.claim: Preventive inspection and maintenance should be performed annually by a service person only.
evidence.evidenceText: The following inspection and maintenance for the unit shall be performed once every year, and handled by a service person only

evidence.claim: Software and firmware updates cannot be performed directly by users in the field.
evidence.evidenceText: the product's software and firmware updates cannot be performed directly by users in the field, and must be performed by authorized service personnel or by returning the product to the manufacturer or service center.

evidence.claim: The product is a medical device and is made in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturing country: Made in Korea This product is a medical device

reviewFlags.reason: Document revision and date are taken from the sourcePath filename only; the body text does not explicitly state Rev 0.5 or document date 260429.
The date string 260429 is not normalized because the source does not state its format.

Authority or jurisdiction is inferred from source references to EU manual context, European Authorized Representative, FCC, FDA guidance, and 21 CFR; no single issuing authority is identified for the manual.
Some figure and table references appear in preprocessed text without images, so label locations, dimensions figures, and waveform figures cannot be fully verified from the extracted text.
